_comment: GM-ONLY reference data for the Solterra: Guildblade template. Full monster stat blocks and scaling rules. Injected into the narrator's reference context; NEVER surfaced in player-facing UI tabs. The player's own discovered-creature notes live in world_state.bestiary (see template.json seed.bestiary).
statBlockFormat: Name (Rank) — VIT · Guard · Atk +X, dmg · Trait · [Flaw] · XP

creatures.name: Goblin Scrapper
creatures.trait: Craven: flees when the pack drops to half.
creatures.notes: M pack = E threat; L warband under a Warboss = D.

creatures.name: Kobold Skitterling
creatures.trait: Trap-setter: the arena starts with one rigged hazard.
creatures.notes: M pack = E; serves Tunnel-Wardens at E.

creatures.name: Giant Rat
creatures.trait: Filth bite: DC 12 CON or −1 to all rolls until rest.
creatures.notes: M+ swarms = E threat.

creatures.name: Gray Slime
creatures.trait: Amorphous: slashing/piercing halved; blunt and fire full.
creatures.notes: Elemental infusion = Elemental Slime (D). See Variants.

creatures.name: Bandit Footpad
creatures.trait: Craven: morale breaks at half numbers.
creatures.notes: M crew = E Cutters; company under a captain = D.

creatures.name: Pirate Deckhand
creatures.trait: Sea legs: no penalty on rigging or wet footing.
creatures.notes: M crew = E Corsairs; ship's company under a Bosun = D.

creatures.name: Razorleap Fish
creatures.trait: Leaping ambush: Advantage round 1 vs targets in or beside water.
creatures.notes: L school = E threat to swimmers and small boats.

creatures.name: Carrion Pecker
creatures.trait: Eye-snatch: on a 19–20, target takes −2 on their next roll.
creatures.notes: Flocks shadow larger predators — a free telegraph.

creatures.name: Mud Snapper
creatures.trait: Shellback: ranged attacks against it at −2.

creatures.name: Tunnel Beetle
creatures.trait: Carapace: the first hit each fight is halved.
creatures.notes: Mana-infused kin appear at E.

creatures.name: Goblin Raider
creatures.trait: Pack tactics: +2 atk while flanking.
creatures.notes: L warband = D; army = C (see Scaling sheet).

creatures.name: Goblin Sparkcaller
creatures.trait: Jolt hex: one target takes −2 while it chants. The chant breaks if it is struck.
creatures.notes: First taste of interruptible casters.

creatures.name: Kobold Tunnel-Warden
creatures.trait: Trapline: springs one prepared hazard per fight.
creatures.notes: L den = D threat.

creatures.name: Bandit Cutter
creatures.trait: Craven: morale breaks at half numbers.
creatures.notes: Company under a captain = D.

creatures.name: Pirate Corsair
creatures.trait: Dirty fighting: one trick per fight — sand, net, bottle.
creatures.notes: Ship's company = D; flotilla = C.

creatures.name: Gnasher
creatures.trait: Pack tactics: +2 atk while flanking. Cowardly alone.
creatures.notes: Led by a Gnasher Alpha = D encounter.

creatures.name: Bog Wisp
creatures.trait: Aether-charged: immune to its own element. Lures travelers off the road.
creatures.notes: Wisp-Mother broods = D.

creatures.name: Sting-Lance Wasp
creatures.trait: Venomous: hit imposes −2 to all rolls until First Aid.
creatures.notes: Hive defense = D threat.

creatures.name: Glimmerwing Moth
creatures.trait: Dazzle dust: DC 14 WIL or Disadvantage next round.

creatures.name: Burrow Mantis
creatures.trait: Ambusher: Advantage on attacks from hiding.

creatures.name: Mire Crawler
creatures.trait: Grasping claws: on hit, grapples (DC 14 STR to break).

creatures.name: Spark-Vein Boar
creatures.trait: Charge: +1d6 on its first hit with a run-up.
creatures.notes: Grows into the Ironback Boar (D).

creatures.name: Ember Slime
creatures.trait: Volatile: detonates on death, 1d8 to adjacent. Amorphous: slash/pierce halved.
creatures.notes: All elemental slimes keep Amorphous from their Gray kin.

creatures.name: Frost Slime
creatures.trait: Chilling mass: melee attackers take −1 to AGI rolls, stacking. Amorphous.

creatures.name: Storm Slime
creatures.trait: Conductive: metal-weapon hits feed back 1d4 to the attacker. Amorphous.

creatures.name: Gnasher Alpha
creatures.trait: Pack howl: once per fight, calls 2 Gnashers.
creatures.flaw: Milk-blind left eye: attacks from its left gain Advantage.
creatures.notes: Stronger E variant — the classic first elite.

creatures.name: Hollow Knight
creatures.trait: Armored: slashing halved. Ancient plate, nothing inside.
creatures.flaw: Gorget gap: a called shot (DC 18) ignores Armored.
creatures.notes: The Hollow King (S) calls every one ever felled.

creatures.name: Marsh Hag
creatures.trait: Withering chant: −2 to player rolls while she chants.
creatures.flaw: Silence or submersion breaks the chant.
creatures.notes: Fully grown = Marsh Hag Matron (C).

creatures.name: Goblin Warboss
creatures.trait: Two actions. Rally: his packs ignore Craven while he stands.
creatures.flaw: Coward's core: one hit of 15+ damage to the face sends him reeling — Guard −2 and Rally dies.
creatures.notes: The boss unit that makes an L warband a D-rank quest.

creatures.name: Pirate Bosun
creatures.trait: Boarding nets: one throw per fight, DC 16 AGI or restrained.
creatures.flaw: Peg leg: footing gambits (grease, ice, listing deck) work against him at Advantage.
creatures.notes: Answers to a Pirate Lord (B) at sea.

creatures.name: Wyvern Fledgling
creatures.trait: Clumsy flight: airborne only every third round.
creatures.notes: Grows into the Ridge Wyvern (C).

creatures.name: Cinder Drake Whelp
creatures.trait: Ember spit: ranged harassment while kin close in.
creatures.notes: Grows into the Cinder Drake (C).

creatures.name: Ironback Boar
creatures.trait: Armored hide: ranged attacks halved.
creatures.flaw: Soft underbelly: attacks while it is reared, tripped, or mid-leap gain +1 damage die.
creatures.notes: Fully grown Spark-Vein Boar.

creatures.name: Hexfen Wisp-Mother
creatures.trait: Brooding: spawns a Bog Wisp each round (max 3 alive).
creatures.flaw: Lantern-tree tether: smash it (VIT 10) — spawning stops and she takes Disadvantage.
creatures.notes: Destructive-level casting begins at this rank.

creatures.name: Blightwood Sapling
creatures.trait: Grasping roots: on hit, may root the target (DC 16 STR).
creatures.flaw: Kindling: fire deals +1d6 against it.
creatures.notes: Grows into the Blightwood Treant (C).

creatures.name: Ridge Wyvern
creatures.trait: Two actions. Skyborne: airborne every other round.
creatures.flaw: Torn wing membrane: 10+ damage to the left wing while grounded keeps it grounded.
creatures.notes: A region-naming terror; the Guildblade doc's sample boss.

creatures.name: Cinder Drake
creatures.trait: Heat haze: ranged attacks against it at Disadvantage.
creatures.flaw: Ember gullet: a shot into the open maw (DC 22 or gambit) ruptures it — 3d8 and no gout for 2 rounds.

creatures.name: Obsidian Basilisk
creatures.trait: Petrifying gaze: DC 18 WIL or −2 AGI, stacking; at −6, stone until cured.
creatures.flaw: Needs eye contact: averted fighting (attacks at Disadvantage) negates it; its own reflection stuns it 1 round.

creatures.name: Granite Golem
creatures.trait: Armored: everything but blunt halved.
creatures.flaw: Rune keystone: INT/Appraisal to spot; a called shot vs Guard 20 staggers it a round.
creatures.notes: The Siege Golem (A) is its great-kin.

creatures.name: Tide-Carved Golem
creatures.trait: Two actions. Undertow stance: drags one fighter 10 ft each round.
creatures.flaw: Keystone, as Granite — but flooded: it is exposed only between waves. Time the strike.

creatures.name: Blightwood Treant
creatures.trait: Two actions. Grasping canopy: ranged attacks out of it at Disadvantage.
creatures.flaw: Kindling heartwood: fire deals +1d8 and it panics, losing an action.
creatures.notes: A fully corrupted grove-heart.

creatures.name: Deepwood Direstag
creatures.trait: Unstoppable charge: ignores the first interception or wall each round.
creatures.flaw: Antler-lock: bait the charge into something solid — stuck for one full round.
creatures.notes: The Wood's wrath wearing a crown.

creatures.name: Marsh Hag Matron
creatures.trait: Two actions. Withering chorus: −2 to all player rolls while she chants.
creatures.flaw: Her reflection: show it to her (mirror, still water) — the chant dies and she takes Disadvantage 1 round.
creatures.notes: Fully grown Marsh Hag (D).

creatures.name: Kraken Whelp
creatures.trait: Many-armed: holds two grapples at once (DC 18 STR to break).
creatures.flaw: Eye cluster: bright light or flash forces it to release and dive.
creatures.notes: A child of the Deep Sea (A).

creatures.name: Reaver Flotilla Captain
creatures.trait: Two actions. Broadside discipline: crew packs attack as one at +2.
creatures.flaw: Mutiny's edge: drop his standard-bearer and every crew checks morale as Craven.
creatures.notes: Lower-tier mob armies in play — see Scaling sheet.

creatures.name: Emberwing Dragon (Young Adult)
creatures.trait: Two actions, true flight. Dragonfear: on first sight, DC 20 WIL or Disadvantage round 1.
creatures.flaw: Newly set scales: a scale knocked loose (crit or gambit) leaves a soft patch — Guard 17 there for the rest of the fight.
creatures.notes: Freshly non-juvenile. Elder form at A. Death redraws the region's power map.

creatures.name: Greater Fire Elemental
creatures.trait: Body of flame: melee attackers take 1d6; immune to fire. Reforms next dawn unless its anchor is destroyed.
creatures.flaw: Anchor brand: bound to a sigil or ley-knot nearby — deface it and it stops reforming, Guard −2.
creatures.notes: Killing the body solves nothing. Find the anchor.

creatures.name: Stormcrown Elemental
creatures.trait: Living storm: ranged shots into it at Disadvantage; it targets metal-bearers first.
creatures.flaw: Anchored to the highest point nearby; grounding-chain gambits bleed 2d10 from it per round.

creatures.name: Thornwrought Knight
creatures.habitat: Old Courts of the Deep Wood
creatures.trait: Geas of the duel: marks one foe; anyone else striking it takes −2.
creatures.flaw: Cold iron: iron weapons deal +1d8 and pierce its glamour outright.
creatures.notes: The Fae courts' silver-rank enforcers.

creatures.name: Druid of the Deep
creatures.trait: Two actions. The Wood fights with them: terrain is hostile; calls one C-rank elite per fight.
creatures.flaw: Oathbound soil: lure them onto dead ground — stone, salt, a ship's deck — and the Wood goes silent: no summons, −2 to all their rolls.
creatures.notes: Not evil. In the way. Or you are.

creatures.name: Pirate Lord of the Sundered Flag
creatures.trait: Two actions. Legendary crew: fights alongside 2 corsair packs that never rout.
creatures.flaw: The Code: invoke parley or formal duel rights and the crews must stand down — then it is just the two of you.
creatures.notes: Death changes the power dynamic of an entire coast.

creatures.name: Ashpelt Tyrant
creatures.trait: Two actions. Territorial dread: lesser beasts flee the region — its approach is always telegraphed.
creatures.flaw: The old harpoon scar: known to local hunters (Tracking or lore); strikes there gain +1 damage die.
creatures.notes: An apex predator is the region's weather.

creatures.name: Saltjaw Reaver
creatures.trait: Two actions. Blood frenzy: +2 atk against any bleeding target.
creatures.flaw: Shallow-bound: lure it onto a shoal or beach it — Guard −4 and it loses its second action.

creatures.name: Elder Dragon
creatures.trait: Three actions, flight, true speech, spellwork. Presence: lesser monsters submit or flee.
creatures.flaw: The First Wound: every elder carries one storied old injury — lore, songs, and quest-arcs reveal where.
creatures.notes: The Coalition mobilizes armies for this.

creatures.name: The First-Grown (an Ancient)
creatures.trait: The land answers: terrain reshapes itself each round.
creatures.flaw: The Old Pact: the true rites of the forgotten people can bind, bargain, or banish — fighting is the worst option.

creatures.name: Vault Guardian
creatures.trait: Protocol body: immune to Flaw exploitation until its condition is met.
creatures.flaw: The entry rite: perform the forgotten people's rite and it stands down. Every Guardian fight is optional — if you solve it.
creatures.notes: Guards what is left of a forgotten people.

creatures.name: Siege Golem
creatures.trait: Rampart-breaker: ignores cover, walls, and structures. Armored: only blunt lands full.
creatures.flaw: Crew furnace: it is fed — foul, starve, or vent the furnace over 3 rounds and it dies standing.
creatures.notes: Built to end cities. Some still walk their last order.

creatures.name: Demon of the Breach
creatures.trait: Two actions + aura. Contract aura: offers real bargains mid-fight — power, for a price.
creatures.flaw: The true name: spoken aloud, Guard −4 and banishment becomes possible. Names live in forbidden ledgers.

creatures.name: The Deep Sea (a Leviathan)
creatures.trait: The sea fights for it: the encounter IS the environment — swells, pull, and dark.
creatures.flaw: It must rise: it only surfaces to feed or to answer blood in the water. Every plan starts with where and when you make it rise.
creatures.notes: Kraken Whelps (C) are its children.

creatures.name: The Pale Khan
creatures.trait: Three actions. Perfect instinct: cannot be ambushed; reads telegraphs — counters the player's declared intent once per round.
creatures.flaw: The old tongue: Beast Speech and the old courtesies can earn parley — peak Beastkin remember being people.
creatures.notes: Wild Beastkin at their absolute peak.

creatures.name: The First Flame Wyrm
creatures.trait: Multi-phase. Its breath remade coastlines, once.
creatures.flaw: Three Flaws, never fewer — each one a full quest-arc to learn.
creatures.notes: Defeating a myth ends a campaign, not a quest.

creatures.name: The Verdant Throne
creatures.trait: The Wood is its body. You have always been inside it.
creatures.flaw: Cannot be fought — only persuaded, poisoned, or replaced. Each is a campaign.
creatures.notes: The Druids of the Deep answer to it.

creatures.name: The Hollow King
creatures.trait: Every Hollow Knight ever felled stands again at its call.
creatures.flaw: The unworn crown: it cannot touch the crown it died for. What that means is the quest.

creatures.name: The Tide That Remembers
creatures.trait: It is the reason sailors pay the old respects.
creatures.flaw: Appeased, never slain — the Sundered Flag knows the rites, and sells them dearly.

creatures.name: The Storm-Sundered Fleet
creatures.trait: A hundred ships that died angry, crewed forever.
creatures.flaw: Strike the flag-debt: settle what the living owe the drowned and it disperses for a generation.

roleTemplates.rule: Band stats, one Trait. Packs share one attack roll at +2 to keep rounds fast.
≈2× band VIT, an extra Trait, and always a Flaw.
≈3× band VIT, two actions per round, a gimmick, and a Flaw. Winnable twice as fast by whoever figures the Flaw out.
Mook VIT, band damage, one battlefield effect. Chants, tethers, and conduits are interruptible by design.
Many bodies, one stat line. AoE, fire, and clever terrain hit the whole line.
Adult's rank −1; ≈ half the adult-band VIT; signature trait in a weaker form.
Multi-phase, multiple Flaws, campaign-scale. The Charter keeps files, not quests.

groupThreatEscalation.note: S scrappers = F quest · M raiding pack = E quest · L warband under a Warboss = D quest · goblin army = C quest.
Quest rank = the highest threat it contains. Pay and quest XP follow quest rank, not body count.

variantTemplates.rule: Juvenile's rank +1: promote all stats to the new band. (Marsh Hag → Matron; Spark-Vein → Ironback.)
Threat +1 rank; +50% VIT; gains one aether Trait AND a corruption Flaw tied to its source.
Apply the Elite template to any mook. Name it. Give it a history and a scar — the scar is the Flaw.
Gray Slime (F) + an element = Elemental Slime (D). Keeps Amorphous; gains the element Trait and a death effect.
Apply the Boss template to a mob's leader to legitimize an L/Army encounter (Warboss, Flotilla Captain, Pirate Lord).

regionalDoctrine.doctrine: Plate work. The Charter's daily bread; new members cut their teeth here.
Destructive casting begins. The first rank at which a single monster can erase a farmstead.
A known, named terror. Regions plan their roads, trade, and seasons around it.
Silver plate (C-rank) required to scout. A full Silver party to engage. Engagement is a Coalition-wide strategic decision; its death redraws the local power map.
Coalition mobilization — armies, heroes, or both. Vaults, elders, the deep places.
Legends and myths. Appeased, outwitted, or endured. Campaign material, never a board posting.

scaling.xpNote: XP is role-flat per Guildblade §7 — F-mook 2 · mook 5 · elite 12 · boss 25 — at any rank. Steepening rank thresholds do the pacing; quest XP and the punching-up bonus (+50%) carry high-rank progression. A- and S-rank victories additionally pay GM milestone XP (suggested 50–150).
